title: Giant Octopus Ruled Cretaceous Seas
summary: For decades, paleontologists believed that the top predators in Late Cretaceous oceans were large vertebrates like mosasaurs, plesiosaurs, and sharks. Invertebrates were considered prey. However, a new study published in Science challenges this view. Researchers have discovered fossilized jaws of a giant octopus, Nanaimoteuthis haggarti, which may have reached lengths of up to 19 meters. Octopuses rarely fossilize because their bodies are soft, but their chitinous beaks can be preserved. The team used a technique called Digital Fossil Mining, which involves grinding rock in microscopic layers and using AI to detect fossils. The beaks showed wear and scratches, indicating active predation. Size estimates were made using allometric calculations based on modern octopuses. This discovery suggests that invertebrates could also be apex predators, reshaping our understanding of ancient food webs.

a method that grinds rock layer by layer and uses AI to find fossils
a method to estimate body size from the size of a body part using growth ratios

background_read: This article discusses a new scientific discovery that challenges previous beliefs about Cretaceous marine food webs. Scientists used an innovative technique to find and analyze octopus fossils. The findings suggest that giant octopuses were top predators, similar to large vertebrates. This shows the importance of new technologies in paleontology.
Article_Structure: Main Points: The article presents the discovery of a giant octopus, Nanaimoteuthis haggarti, which challenges the view that only vertebrates were apex predators in Cretaceous oceans. Purpose: To inform readers about a paradigm shift in paleontology regarding the role of invertebrates. Evidence Evaluation: The evidence is based on fossilized beaks found using a novel technique, but size estimates rely on allometric calculations with a range. Author Credibility: The study is published in Science and led by a paleontologist from Hokkaido University. Methodology: Digital Fossil Mining (grinding tomography) and AI detection were used.

perspectives.description: Vertebrates like mosasaurs were the top predators.
Giant octopuses could also be apex predators.
